key_insight: Elastic strain energy reduction can drive abnormal grain growth in ultrafine-grained Ni thin films, selectively favoring grains with in-plane <100> orientation under cyclic loading.
engineering_relevance: The study suggests that even purely elastic service conditions can trigger microstructural coarsening, which matters for designing grain-refined metals with stable mechanical performance.
method: Phase-field simulations of grain growth under cyclic loading
Micromechanical analysis of local stress states and grain geometry
Comparative study across materials with different elastic anisotropy
evidence_signal: The abstract reports simulation results that reproduce AGG kinetics and show orientation-selective growth consistent with experimentally observed <100>-aligned grains.
watch_item: The mechanism is demonstrated computationally, so validating how broadly it applies across real alloys, thicknesses, and loading histories remains important.
paper_title: Phase-field modeling of elastically driven abnormal grain growth
